title: Tapping to Calm Body Stress and Accept Now
subtitle: An EFT sequence for releasing the body tension that comes from resisting where you are
excerpt: An EFT tapping sequence for the stress that accumulates when you are at war with your body: pushing past signals, comparing what you have with what you want, finding the body insufficient. Taps through the resistance and toward acceptance as a practical starting point.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education, with reference to the body-listening and acceptance framework in WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are in resistance to the body as it is — pushing through signals, comparing it to what it was or what you want it to be.

content.text: An EFT tapping sequence for the stress that comes from being at war with your body. The body that doesn't look how you want it to, doesn't do what you push it to, doesn't match the image of what it should be. This sequence works on the resistance. Not to abandon the desire to change, but to start from where you actually are.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I struggle to accept my body as it is right now, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the body I have and the body I want feel very far apart, I accept this is where I am right now.
Even though I've been at war with my body for a long time, I am willing to try a different approach today.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The ways my body doesn't look how I want it to.
Side of Eye: The comparison with what it used to be.
Under Eye: The ways I push past signals I should listen to.
Under Nose: The frustration of a body that isn't cooperating.
Chin: The gap between the body I have and the body I want.
Collarbone: The stress I carry from being in resistance to it.
Under Arm: The exhaustion of this particular war.
Top of Head: Being in a body I am not fully at peace with.
Reframe
Eyebrow: This body is doing something. It has been doing something every day.
Side of Eye: The comparison with the past is a story, not a measurement of what I have now.
Under Eye: The signals are information I can learn to read rather than override.
Under Nose: Acceptance doesn't mean I stop wanting to change. It means I start from here.
Chin: The relationship I want with my body starts with less resistance, not more.
Collarbone: I can give my body something it needs today. Even one thing.
Under Arm: The war costs more than the peace. I can try a ceasefire.
Top of Head: I begin from where I am. Here is where I am.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

Written for homemade.education, drawing on the body-listening and acceptance framework in WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
